title: Write a Collection requirements.yml
description: Create a requirements.yml that installs specific versions of community.general, amazon.aws, and a private collection, then write a playbook using FQCN from those collections.
starterCode:
# Write a requirements.yml that:
# 1. Installs community.general version >= 7.0.0
# 2. Installs amazon.aws at exactly version 6.5.0
# 3. Installs kubernetes.core version >= 3.0.0
# The file must have a 'collections:' top-level key

# After the requirements.yml, add a comment separator
# Then write a playbook task that uses community.general.git_config
# to set user.email to 'ci@example.com' (scope: global)
solutionCode:
collections:
  - name: community.general
    version: '>=7.0.0'
  - name: amazon.aws
    version: '6.5.0'
  - name: kubernetes.core
    version: '>=3.0.0'
---
- name: Use collections
  hosts: localhost
  connection: local
  tasks:
    - name: Configure git user email
      community.general.git_config:
        name: user.email
        value: ci@example.com
        scope: global
hints: requirements.yml uses 'collections:' as the top-level key (not 'roles:')
Always use FQCN (namespace.collection.module) when calling collection modules

validationRules.errorMessage: Add a 'collections:' top-level key in requirements.yml
Include community.general in the collections list
Include amazon.aws in the collections list
Use FQCN (community.general.module_name) in your playbook task
YAML is invalid — check collection list formatting

simulatedOutput.success:
Starting galaxy collection install process
Process install dependency map
Starting collection install process
Installing 'community.general:7.5.0' to '/home/user/.ansible/collections'
Installing 'amazon.aws:6.5.0' to '/home/user/.ansible/collections'
Installing 'kubernetes.core:3.1.0' to '/home/user/.ansible/collections'

Playbook task validated: FQCN usage confirmed.
simulatedOutput.failure: Requirements validation failed. Check 'collections:' key, version constraints, and FQCN usage.
